ownership_note: Per the team lead, all 5 defects are confined to agent/documents/document_qa/ (retrieval.py). A sibling builder (corr2) owns the other documents sub-clusters (layout/report/intelligence, extraction/table_text, deal_summary), so this task touches NONE of those. service.py in document_qa/ is async (settings + Qdrant + Mongo) and not hermetically testable, so all five defects live in retrieval.py, which imports only `re` + typing.
naming_note: Originally assigned slug `latent-voice-calls`; the voice/telephony surface is ~all async orchestration with too few pinned pure-math sites, so the lead approved a pivot to document_qa. Deliverable slug = latent-doc-qa.
difficulty_calibration: 2 EASY + 3 MEDIUM. All five are single-token edits on existing lines with no new imports/helpers/comments, spread across five statements in three functions of retrieval.py. Shapes: operator >=/> (cap-phrase length), operator </<= (label length), function swap max()->min() (dedup), operator >=/> (fact-count cap), threshold value 3->4 (term length). No defect is a convention-pick (every boundary is pinned by an adjacent sibling, the module's own regex, or cap/dedup semantics). The visible suite is byte-for-byte green at base+5-defects (same 7 pre-existing rot failures as clean HEAD), so every defect must be found with no failing-test gradient. Repeated shape-TYPES (two >=/>) sit on genuinely different expressions/functions/derivations (phrase length pinned by regex-minimum vs fact-count cap pinned by limit-semantics) so no single grep collapses them — mirrors the approved latent-financial-tools, which shipped two </<= and two value-changes.
partial_detail: Reverting 2 of 5 defects (D2 label length, D3 dedup max) leaves 3 f2p still FAILED (13/16 required passed) -> reward 0.
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN - identical failure set to clean HEAD. Clean loangenus-repo:v1 and the five-defect built image BOTH report 7 failed / 166 passed / 24 skipped, the 7 being the same pre-existing rot (test_inbound_calls x3, test_document_ingestion_readiness x2, test_document_report x1, test_ingestion_stale_recovery x1). Zero new failures introduced.
env_note: No task-specific env. retrieval.py imports only `re` + typing (no agent.core.config.settings, no DB/Qdrant/network), so it loads cleanly in-image with PYTHONPATH=/app/loangen-agent and the baked JWT_SECRET_KEY suffices. No pydantic Settings ValidationError. Gold tests are pure-input (str/list/dict/scalar in, list/dict/scalar out) with zero mocks.

defects.boundary_changed: if len(phrase) >= 5  ->  if len(phrase) > 5  (minimum capitalized multi-word phrase length)
defects.edge_input: a question containing a two-word capitalized phrase at the shortest producible length, e.g. "the My Co portfolio filing" ("My Co" is 5 chars)
defects.wrong_output: the minimal phrase is dropped from the returned phrases (['my co'] -> []); a real multi-word phrase is lost from keyword scoring
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: test_extracts_capitalized_phrase feeds "Real Estate Income" (18 chars); only the exactly-5-char case flips.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE, medium (regex-minimum reasoning) - the capitalized-sequence pattern `[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+` two lines above cannot match anything shorter than 5 chars (two 2-letter words + one space), so >= 5 admits every real match and > 5 uniquely drops the minimal ones.

defects.boundary_changed: if len(label) < 3  ->  if len(label) <= 3  (minimum meaningful label length)
defects.edge_input: a statement line whose label is exactly three characters, e.g. "NOI  250000.00"
defects.wrong_output: the labelled amount is dropped (returns {} instead of {"noi": 250000.0}); a valid 3-char label (NOI, PPE, etc.) is discarded
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: test_labeled_amounts_from_pfs_text uses labels of length 12/18; no running test feeds a 3-char label.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE, easy - the module's own `_LABELED_AMOUNT_RE` label group `.{3,80}` sets the minimum accepted label length to 3, and the sibling quoted-phrase / search-term thresholds `>= 3` in extract_search_terms (same file, left correct at L145) confirm 3 as the minimum meaningful length, so < 3 is the correct guard.

defects.boundary_changed: existing.vector_score = max(existing.vector_score, hit.vector_score)  ->  min(...)  (keep the stronger score when a chunk is surfaced twice)
defects.edge_input: the same chunk (identical ChunkRef) present twice with different vector scores, e.g. 0.5 and 0.9
defects.wrong_output: the merged chunk keeps the weaker score (0.5 instead of 0.9); a chunk retrieved by two signals is under-scored and can be mis-ranked / dropped from top_k
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: test_merge_promotes_keyword_hit_missing_from_vector_top uses three DISTINCT keys, so the duplicate-merge branch is never exercised.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE, medium (adjacent sibling + semantics) - the very next line merges keyword_score with `max(...)` (left correct); the two dedup lines must be symmetric, and reciprocal-rank-fusion dedup keeps the strongest evidence, so the vector line must also be max.

defects.boundary_changed: if len(facts) >= limit  ->  if len(facts) > limit  (stop once the fact set reaches its cap)
defects.edge_input: more labelled lines than the cap, e.g. three lines with limit=2
defects.wrong_output: collects limit + 1 facts instead of exactly limit (3 instead of 2); the documented maximum is exceeded by one
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: test_labeled_amounts_from_pfs_text feeds 2 lines under the default limit of 40; no running test fills the fact set to its cap.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE, medium (cap semantics) - `limit` is the maximum number of facts to collect; once the set already holds `limit` entries the next must be rejected, so the guard breaks at `>= limit`. `> limit` lets a (limit + 1)-th fact through, violating the cap.

defects.edge_input: a question containing a three-letter term, e.g. "ltv and roi trends"
defects.wrong_output: three-letter terms are dropped from the keyword terms (['ltv','roi','trends'] -> ['trends']); real acronyms (LTV, ROI, NOI, DTI) never reach keyword scoring
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: test_extracts_capitalized_phrase asserts "income" (6 chars) is a term; no running test feeds a 3-char term.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE, easy - the sibling quoted-phrase threshold `if len(phrase) >= 3` in the same function (left correct) uses 3 as the minimum meaningful token length, and a 3-letter financial acronym is unambiguously a real search term, so >= 3 is the correct floor.

not_planted_left_correct.status: left correct - the sibling anchor that pins defects 2 (label min) and 5 (term min) to a minimum meaningful length of 3
left correct - the adjacent sibling that pins defect 3 (vector dedup must also be max)

fairness_audit: All five f2p and eleven p2p tests are pure-input (str/list/dict/scalar in, list/dict/scalar out) and use zero mocks, so no collaborator patching can encode the oracle's implementation. Each f2p asserts the behavior at the exact edge, and each edge has a uniquely-derivable correct side (capitalized-sequence regex minimum = 5 pins >= 5; the regex `.{3,80}` + the left-correct quoted-phrase sibling `>= 3` pins the 3-char label and the 3-char term; the adjacent keyword-score `max()` + RRF dedup semantics pin `max` for the vector score; `limit` = maximum pins `>= limit`). Each p2p pins a just-off-edge value that no correct fix changes (four-char and two-char terms; six-char and eighteen-char phrases; four-char and two-char labels; all-facts-under-limit and limit-equal-to-count; keyword-score dedup, single-occurrence, and distinct-chunk cases). No test asserts a convention-choice boundary. Alternative-implementation audit: rewriting any target with different control flow (an early-continue vs a filter comprehension for the length guards, or an explicit if/else for the dedup) returns the same asserted outputs at every f2p and p2p edge.
resistance_assessment: Targeting the Opus 1-6/10 band. The lever is derivation difficulty with no failing-test gradient: the full visible suite is byte-for-byte green at base+5-defects (same 7 pre-existing rot failures as clean HEAD), inside a single 533-line retrieval module with many plausible length/threshold comparisons, so no test bisects and an agent must read and reason about each boundary. Two easy defects are pinned by an in-file sibling / the module's own regex; three medium ones require regex-minimum reasoning (D1), spotting an asymmetry with the adjacent sibling plus understanding RRF dedup (D3), or cap semantics (D4). An agent that fixes only the literal reported symptoms (the instruction gives 3 of 5 as prose examples) still misses D4 (the count cap) and can mis-fix D1 (>= 5 reads plausibly as '>' ). Breadth: 5 f2p across 3 functions. Interaction: D2 (label min) and D5 (term min) both encode 'minimum meaningful length = 3' with different shapes and different anchors, so an agent that generalises one insight must still apply it in two directions. All five are single-token edits; two share the >=/> shape-type but on different expressions/functions.
litmus_result: PASS. Given only the instruction (the document_qa retrieval helpers under loangen-agent/agent/documents/document_qa/, symptom = wrong results on short terms/phrases, short labels, capped result sets, and duplicate-chunk merges; no function names, boundary directions, values, counts, or defect shape), a strong dev cannot locate the five defect lines in <2 minutes: the surface is a 533-line hybrid-retrieval module where every length/threshold/cap comparison is a plausible boundary, no test bisects, and the changes are single tokens. Two boundaries are then cleanly derivable (in-file sibling / regex); three are medium (regex-minimum reasoning; adjacent-sibling asymmetry + RRF; cap semantics). Breadth (5 f2p across 3 functions) and the D2/D5 min-length interaction both hold.
